name: New Student & Family Programs
description:
New Student & Family Programs (NSFP) offers programs throughout the year to help you transition to TCU.

In the summer, you start by attending Orientation, Frog Camp and Frogs First. Students continue their journey through First-Year Leadership Opportunities (FLOs), registering for the one-hour course Introduction to University Life (UNLF) and attending First-Year Friday events throughout the semester.

name: Gymnastics Club
description: TCU Gymnastics is a fun, inclusive, and energetic space where students of all skill levels and athletic backgrounds can come together to move, learn, and connect. Our recreational team is all about learning new skills at no stakes. For those who want to take things to the next level, we also have a competitive team that has proudly brought home multiple national titles! We compete statewide and nationally within TGC (Texas Gymnastics Conference) under NAIGC (National Association of Intercollegiate Gymnastics). We practice Mondays and Wednesdays from 7 PM - 9 PM at Lone Star Gymnastics off-campus.
summary: Whether you’re a former gymnast or just want to try something new, TCU Gymnastics is a co-ed club for all current TCU students! Join us for a fun, no-pressure way to stay active, learn skills, and meet awesome people. Come flip out with us!

name: Italian Club
description:
Texas Christian University’s (TCU) Italian Club celebrates and immerses students in Italian language, culture, and traditions. Our organization actively fosters a deep appreciation for Italy's heritage through engaging and educational events on and off campus.

We regularly host Italian cooking classes, movie nights, and lively conversation hours where members can practice their Italian in an informal setting. Additionally, we participate in cultural excursions, such as attending performances at the Dallas Opera and exploring the Kimbell Art Museum.

Many TCU students participate in study abroad programs in Italy (TCU offers programs in Firenze, Roma, and Siracusa). Italian Club prepares students for this endeavor by bridging the gap between academic learning and real-world experiences. We aim to create a dynamic and interactive environment that brings Italian culture to life for our members and the broader community.

GroupMe link: https://groupme.com/join_group/103601387/mlGIZhDi
summary: The purpose of this organization is to celebrate Italian language, culture, and traditions. This organization hosts weekly fun and informative events and attends local cultural showcases, such as the Dallas Opera and the Kimbell Art Museum.

Today, Lambda prides itself on its multi-cultural brotherhood of gentlemen from different creeds, nationalities, and religious beliefs. Our brothers, nationwide, stay true to our mottos “En la union, esta la fuerza”/”In unity, there is strength” and “Chivalry Above Self,” and set the precedent of what it means to be fraternal men, gentlemen, and role models in the community.

name: TCU Energy Club
description:
The TCU Energy Club is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring the dynamic and evolving world of energy. Open to all majors, our mission is to provide students with opportunities to learn, connect, and engage with leaders across the energy industry.

Each month, the club hosts two guest speakers from diverse areas of the energy sector—ranging from oil and gas to renewables, technology, and finance—giving members direct insight into current trends, challenges, and innovations. In addition, we organize field trips to industry sites, allowing students to see energy operations up close and apply classroom knowledge to real-world settings.

We also join monthly lunches at the Petroleum Club of Fort Worth, where members are encouraged to join and connect with professionals, alumni, and executives in an intimate setting. These gatherings expand professional connections and provide a deeper understanding of career pathways in energy.

Whether you’re curious about the fundamentals of energy, interested in the future of sustainability, or preparing for a career in the industry, the TCU Energy Club offers a welcoming community where ideas, opportunities, and professional growth thrive.
summary: The mission of Texas Christian University Energy Club is to create stimulated communication between students and industry leaders in a way that both involves and educates the next generation of the energy business.

name: Student Government Association
description:
If you are interested in joining SGA, please visit https://sga.tcu.edu/get-involved/ 

 

Since 1914, the Student Government Association has worked to fulfill its mission: to represent the collective voice of the student body while encompassing the spirit of the TCU community through service, programming and legislation. SGA is comprised of four branches:

 

1) Legislative: The House of Student Representatives gives the student body a voice by authoring and voting on legislation designed to improve life at TCU.

 

2) Executive: The Executive Branch is the main body responsible for leading Student Government and communicating with the student body. This branch is composed of the Student Body Officers and Cabinet.

 

3) Programming: theCrew and theEnd fund, plan, and execute a variety of activities and events on-campus, such as Frogs First, Family Weekend, Homecoming, Holidays at TCU, concerts, movie nights and much more.

 

4) Judicial The Judicial Board listens to and decides on matters relating to the violation of Student Government’s governing documents.
summary:
It is the mission of SGA to represent the collective voice of the student body while encompassing the spirit of the TCU community through service, programming and legislation.

Visit https://sga.tcu.edu/get-involved/ for information on how to join

We are a student-run organization consisting of two teams that travel to tournaments around the state of Texas during the regular season to compete against other collegiate club volleyball teams. The top team, Purple, will occasionally travel out of the state during the regular season to get stronger competition before the National tournament, which is the last tournament of the season at a location that changes yearly. Regardless of the team, together we are a competitive sports organization that works hard to get the TCU name around the world of collegiate club volleyball.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta Texas Zeta Chapter
description:
Join our GroupMe with the link -->  CLICK HERE

Follow our Instagram with the link --> CLICK HERE

 

The Texas Zeta Chapter of AED has its roots as THE original TCU Premed/Pre-Health Honor Society, which was formed in 1975 with Dr. Manfred Reinecke as its faculty advisor. The society expanded to the point that, in 1978, a committee was formed to petition for official affiliation with the national organization AED.

The National AED office approved the petition, and on April 20, 1979, the Texas Zeta chapter of AED was established at TCU with the initiation of 31 active members, three active alumni, and five honorary members. Dr. Reinecke served as the faculty advisor until 1991, followed by Dr. Phil Hartman. Dr. Matt Chumchal succeeded Dr. Hartman in 2012 and served as the interim faculty advisor, officially taking the position in 2013. Dr. Chumchal currently resides as the chair of the TCU's Health Professions Committee, and Dr. Reinecke and Dr. Hartman have also served as chairs. Both Dr. Reinecke and Dr. Hartman have served as Regional Directors to the national organization.

Since the fall of 2019, AED has been a student-run organization and continues to thrive as an independent organization. 

Check out our website, https://hornedfrogsaed.wixsite.com/txzeta/join, for further details on our Club Membership vs the National Membership requirements and how to get involved in AED at TCU!!

 

-The objectives of AED at TCU are to:

* Encourage and recognize excellence in Pre-Health scholarship and dedication

* Stimulate an appreciation for our Pre-Health education and faculty

* Promote cooperation and contact between health professions students, premedical students, and educators to develop an amazing program of Pre-Health education.

* Bind together similarly interested Pre-Health students

* Use its knowledge to benefit health organizations, charities, and the local Fort Worth community.

name: Ensemble en Francais
description:
Bienvenue à Ensemble en Français! 

 

We are an organization that promotes the linguistic and cultural exploration of the countries around the world where French is spoken, from France and beyond. Did you know that there are 50 countries where French is fluently spoken? Through our activities on campus, we hope to connect students to the francophone world! We offer engaging events to increase awareness of the culture and language of francophone countries, and to demonstrate the value of learning French in today's global society.

 

Ensemble en Français is open to all students, and you're welcome to join us even if you don't speak French! Do you have an interest in the francophone world? Do you speak French, or want to learn more about the French language? Do you enjoy French food? Ensemble en Français is the place for you, then!

Members are actively engaged in events on campus and in the community which include participation in health fairs, distribution of health education information, collection drives for shelters, and collaborating with other organizations. name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated (AKA) is an international service organization that was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek-letter organization established by African-American college-educated women. Alpha Kappa Alpha is comprised of nearly 300,000 members in approximately 1,018 graduate and undergraduate chapters in the United States, the U.S. Virgin Islands,  the Bahamas, Bermuda, Canada, Dubai, Germany, Japan, Liberia, and South Korea. Led by International President Dr. Glenda Baskin Glover, Alpha Kappa Alpha is often hailed as “America’s premier Greek-letter organization for African American women.” For more information on Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority and its programs, log onto www.aka1908.com.
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated is a Greek-letter organization established by and for college women. Established in 1908, it is the oldest Greek-letter organization founded by Black women. Membership is open to undergraduates and graduates.

name: Sigma Phi Lambda
description:
Sigma Phi Lambda (ΣΦΛ)

is a Christian sorority that strives to glorify God by providing a source of fellowship for college women who sincerely seek to know His person, His will, and His ways. It is a non-denominational organization of sisters where fellowship with our most high God is fostered by growth in unity with one another. Through weekly meetings that include worship, encouragement, fellowship, and accountability, Phi Lamb provides an alternative to Panhellenic sororities. Sigma Phi Lambda is not presently, nor will it ever be Panhellenic. Our heart and passion can be summed up in our sorority verse, Romans 15:5-6:

"May the God who gives endurance and encouragement give you a spirit of unity among yourselves as you follow Christ Jesus so that with one heart and one mouth you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ."

Our Purpose

Sigma Phi Lambda exists for the sole purpose of glorifying our Lord Jesus Christ and making His name great.

What We Believe

Our statement of belief is the Apostles' Creed, one of the oldest and most unifying statements of faith in the history of the Christian faith:

I believe in God, the Father almighty, creator of heaven and earth.
I believe in Jesus Christ, His only Son, our Lord.
He was conceived by the power of the Holy Spirit and born of the Virgin Mary.
He suffered under Pontius Pilate, was crucified, died and was buried.
He descended to the dead. On the third day, he rose again.
He ascended into heaven and is seated at the right hand of the Father.
He will come again to judge the living and the dead.
I believe in the Holy Spirit, in the holy catholic* Church,
the communion of saints, the forgiveness of sins,
the resurrection of the body, and the life everlasting. Amen
*The word catholic is used in this context to mean universal.
summary: We are a non-denominational Christian sorority separate from Panhellenic sororities. We are a group of women forming a Christian sisterhood that provides fellowship and community throughout college. Our main goal is to make God's name great on campus!

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description:
Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. (ΣΓΡ) was founded on November 12, 1922, at Butler University in Indianapolis, Indiana by seven young educators. Founded in the midst of segregation, Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. is the only sorority of the four historically African-American sororities in the National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) established at a PWI. The sorority aims to enhance the quality of life within the community through public service, leadership development, and education of youth. 

The Tau Rho chapter of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. was chartered on April 29, 2018 by 9 illustrious young women on the campus of Texas Christian University (TCU). Sorors of Tau Rho champion academic excellence and work to promote the values and standards of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority on TCU’s campus. During its inaugural year, Tau Rho set the stage for its legacy and impact within Fort Worth and the surrounding areas through events concerning education, mental health, breast cancer awareness, and notable women in Black history.

If you would like to learn more about Sigma Gamma Rho and Tau Rho, then please contact us at tcusgrho@gmail.com.

Get to know Sigma Gamma Rho!
summary: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. (ΣΓΡ) was founded on November 12, 1922, at Butler University in Indianapolis, Indiana by seven young educators. The Tau Rho chapter was chartered at TCU on April 29, 2018 by nine trailblazing young women.

name: African Student Organization
description:
The African Student Organization (ASO) is all about connection, culture, and community. We create a space where students can celebrate African heritage, learn from one another, and see how our stories and traditions shape the world around us. At ASO, African heritage isn’t just about being African. It’s about embracing your roots, loving where you come from, and sharing that pride with others who are eager to learn. Through conversations, weekly events, and cultural exchange, we bring together diverse worldviews while highlighting Africa’s impact on global society.

Here's how YOU can get involved:

- Request membership on TCU Engage.

- Follow us on Instagram: @tcu_aso

- Join our 2025/2026 GroupMe!

 

Get to know the African Student Organization!

 

Mission: To unite, create a common bond, and give voice to all students of African heritage through inclusive community-building.  

Vision: To create meaningful opportunities for engagement, dialogue, and collaboration that highlight the richness and diversity of African cultures, fostering a more globally-minded and culturally competent TCU community. We envision a TCU community where the vibrancy of African cultures is celebrated, respected, and integrated into the fabric of campus life, promoting cross-cultural understanding and solidarity.
summary: To create meaningful opportunities for engagement, dialogue, and collaboration that highlight the richness and diversity of African cultures, fostering a more globally-minded and culturally competent TCU community.

description:
Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated is the first and largest Latin sorority in the nation, founded in December 1975 at Kean University in Union, New Jersey, by 17 visionary women.
Originally created to provide a support system for Latina women in higher education, Lambda Theta Alpha has grown into an inclusive organization that empowers women of all backgrounds.
Guided by our principles of Unity, Love, and Respect, we strive to uplift our communities through academic excellence, leadership, and service.
Our motto, “Latin by Tradition, not by Definition,” reflects our belief in embracing diversity while honoring our roots.
The Epsilon Rho Chapter proudly continues this legacy at Texas Christian University since 2011, fostering sisterhood and positive impact across campus and beyond.

name: Frogs for Autism
description:
Frogs for Autism works to engage the campus and local community in awareness, advocacy and fundraising efforts to help individuals and families affected by autism spectrum disorders.

Our organization is in partnership with a local nonprofit school for learning differences called Wedgwood Academy. Frogs for Autism holds monthly Respite Nights at Wedgwood to allow members of our organization to spend time with students with special needs and/or learning differences for a "parent's night off." At the Respite, TCU students are paired with a Wedgwood Academy student and are given free range of fun activities at the school to build relationships and have fun with students. 

In the times in between Respite Nights, Frogs for Autism holds club meetings and profit shares to fundraise for Wedgwood and participate in other community events related to ASD.
summary: Frogs for Autism works to engage the campus and local community in awareness, advocacy and fundraising efforts to help individuals and families affected by autism spectrum disorders.

name: BNSF Neeley Leadership Program
description:
The BNSF Neeley Leadership Program is a three-year, cohort-based program designed to develop leaders who look at challenges and solutions from a variety of viewpoints, value collaboration, and develop ideas that drive the world forward. Cohorts of approximately 30 students participate in classroom and experiential learning, centered around the BNSF Neeley Leadership Competency Model. The unrivaled curriculum of the program includes five leadership courses across the sophomore, junior, and senior years.

NLP students Step Up to leadership roles. They Step In to support their peers and organizations. They develop along the Neeley Leader Competency Model to become Neeley Leaders.

Neeley Leaders have a Learner and Growth Mindset. They embrace opportunities to develop themselves. They welcome challenging conversations and are open to the idea that they may not always be right.

Neeley Leaders demonstrate High Integrity. They hold themselves and peers accountable. They measure success based on a higher standard.

Neeley Leaders are Strong Organizational Citizens. They value their relationships within NLP and are quick to help. They speak up in the face of wrongdoing and make NLP an environment where everyone can thrive.
summary:
Leaders at work. Leaders in life.

BNSF Neeley Leadership Program (NLP): We prepare leaders who enable people and organizations to meet the needs of an ever-changing, complex world.

name: TCU Tunnel of Expression
description:
Tunnel of Expression, formerly known as Tunnel of Oppression, is an interactive event that highlights contemporary issues of oppression. It is designed to introduce participants to concepts of oppression, privilege, and power. Participants are guided through a series of scenes that aim to educate and challenge them to think more deeply about issues of oppression. At the end of the tour, participants are provided with the opportunity to discuss their experiences with each other. Facilitators help participants reflect on their experiences and to put their new-found knowledge to use in their everyday lives.

Room Sponsor Form

Participant sign-up  

Volunteers will assist in various roles during their shift to support the functioning of Tunnel.

Sign-up to volunteer here

Facilitators will join participants through the Tunnel and lead a debrief conversations with participants following their experience.

summary: Tunnel of Expression is an interactive event that highlights contemporary issues of oppression.  It is designed to introduce participants to concepts of oppression, privilege, and power.

name: Sigma Nu
description:
The Sigma Nu Lambda Epsilon chapter was rechartered in 2016 and was awarded back to back TCU IFC chapter of the year awards in 2023 and 2024. At 175 active members as of last semester, it is the largest fraternity on TCU’s campus. Sigma Nu has recently been honored with a fourth-consecutive Rock Chapter award from headquarters, naming Lambda Epsilon as one of the best Sigma Nu chapters in the country. Their core values are love, honor, and truth, and they are stoked to welcome their 2024 Candidate class this fall. Here are some highlights:

- Brotherhood events: Mavericks, Stars, and Panther City games, super bowl watch parties, barbecues, tourneys, pick up games, and spring break trips
- Philanthropy: Partnership with Wounded Warrior Project, Saving Hope Animal Rescue, and Fort Worth Miracle League
- Service: Sigma Nu Serves Day, Boys and Girls Club, Tarrant County Food Bank
- Social: Austin White Rose Formal and various mixers, semi-formals, and tailgates
- 100% Brother Involvement Rate in Campus Orgs
- Huge participation in the Neeley Fellows Program, Neeley Leadership Program, Sales Program, TCU SGA, and ROTC
- Indoor and Outdoor IM Soccer Champs, Club Lacrosse, Club Soccer, Rugby
- Initiation and annual events at Brother Chancellor Boschini’s Mansion
- Chapter GPA: 3.56

Check out @tcusigmanu on Instagram to learn more about the Lambda Epsilon chapter.
summary: Sigma Nu Lambda Epsilon chapter was rechartered in 2016 and is TCU IFC 2023 and 2024 chapter of the year. At 175 active members as of last semester, it is the largest fraternity on TCU’s campus. As a 4-time Rock chapter, we value love, honor, and truth.

The program challenges top students to stretch beyond their already competitive undergraduate coursework in business and provides opportunities to interact with top faculty and business executives both inside and outside of the classroom. The Neeley Fellows Program recognizes excellence and rewards it through exciting out-of-class growth opportunities for students. The program begins in the sophomore year. Selected students participate as cohorts in a broad range of activities designed to integrate practical skills with theoretical learning resulting in an enriched educational experience. Students build long lasting professional relationships and friendships.

We offer classes in many different styles of dance including, but not limited to, jazz, hip hop, ballet, tap, contemporary, modern, lyrical, musical theater, and improv. name: Real Estate Club
description:
About Us

Whether you're passionate about commercial real estate or curious about residential properties, the TCU Real Estate Club is the perfect place to explore your interests and jump-start your career. We host weekly meetings most Tuesdays at 5:00 p.m. in Neeley.

name: Rowing Club
description:
This team represents TCU at regattas throughout Texas, Oklahoma, and elsewhere. As we continue to expand TCU's presence in the prestigious sport of Rowing, we are always looking for new opportunities to improve the team to quickly become the best in our region and beyond.

With excellent training from coaches and ample facilities students will improve their skill in rowing, or learn from scratch if inexperienced. Both experienced rowers and athletes with no prior rowing experience are welcome and will have the opportunity to compete in multiple regattas during the fall and springs seasons.

The start of the season is most intense. Experienced rowers will undergo reconditioning to get back into rowing shape. Students new to the sport will go through basic training on and off the water to learn how to row, while also building up their conditioning via the rowing machine and TCU Rec Facilities. First regatta is in Oklahoma City apx. one month into the Fall semester, with a practice regatta earlier on. If experienced, you will love the opportunity to get better and faster for TCU. If new to the sport, you will learn a competitive team sport that will forever impact your college experience and beyond.

TCU Rowing is a tight-knit team that bonds through early morning or late afternoon water practices, rowing machine conditioning sessions, non-rowing related activities, and exciting opportunities to beat other universities and rowing organizations on the water.

If you are athletic, enjoy trying new challenges, willing to learn, dedicated to team goals, and eager to compete for TCU, you will be a valuable member of the TCU Rowing family!

Row Frogs!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I9oogbijO18
summary: This team represents TCU at regattas throughout Texas, Oklahoma, and elsewhere. We continue to expand TCU's presence in the prestigious sport of Rowing; always looking for new opportunities to improve to quickly become the best in our region and beyond!

Delta Sigma Pi was founded in 1907 at New York University: School of Commerce, Accounts and Finance by Harold Valentine Jacobs, Alexander Frank Makay, Alfred Moysello and Henry Albert Tienken. name: Omega Delta Phi Fraternity Inc.
description: The purpose of this Brotherhood, a Service/Social fraternity dedicated to the needs and concerns of the community, is and shall be to promote and maintain the traditional values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity, and Leadership. This Brotherhood was founded in order to provide, to ANY man, a diverse fraternal experience which coincides with a higher education. Since founding in 1987, Omega Delta Phi has been an organization perpetually dedicated to the idea of constant growth and improvement. We must work to form an organization which not only challenges the traditional perception of fraternities, but also deals with the issues of culture and race in a completely new perspective. Omega Delta Phi is a multicultural service/social fraternity that aims at graduating its members while giving back to the community. The Fraternity has established itself on 50+ campuses and is predominantly centered in Texas and the Southwest. However, the Fraternity has been experiencing tremendous growth in the Midwest the last 10 years. Although founded primarily by Latinos, the Fraternity has traditionally always been open to men of different backgrounds, as demonstrated through its “One culture, any race” philosophy.

description:
Disciples on Campus (DOC) is a TCU campus ministry affiliated with The Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) and open to all. Through DOC, students have an opportunity to embody what it means to be a movement for wholeness in a fragmented world. As such, we commit ourselves to the following priorities:

Community

We foster real relationships marked by curiosity, authenticity, and vulnerability.

Inclusion

We are open to and affirming of the LGBTQ+ community, and we welcome all to Jesus' table as He has so freely welcomed us.

Justice

We love all people and advocate for the vulnerable and mistreated.

Joy

We cultivate peace and happiness in one another and the world around us.

 

WEEKLY PROGRAMMING

Monday Fun Day, 6pm

Join us for a time to relax, unwind, and have fun! Location varies. Check the Instagram for details!

DOC Groups

name: Anthropological Society
description:
The Anthropology Department at TCU studies the four branches of anthropology:
1. Social anthropology may be of interest to those studying administration, human resources, and public health as well as other related fields.

2. Linguistics is of interest if you wish to learn more about culture, language, and creative writing among other things.

3. Biological anthropology encompasses medical, forensics, osteology, and anatomy.

4. Archaeology is the study of humans of the past, history, and the changes of human behavior over time among other things.

If you wish to bring the human aspects of your field into your career, then the Anthropological Society invites you to join us as we strive to take what we learn from Anthropology courses and put the into practice. For example, biology can study how cancer behaves and how to treat it. Anthropology in medicine is concerned with that as well as the human aspects of illness.

Questions in this case would include:

Can the person seeking treatment afford medical care?

Does this person live in a location where medical care is accessible?

How does someone's culture play in role in their seeking medical attention, and what restrictions might they have compared to others?

Does this person work long hours which would prevent them from taking medication on time and with a meal?

If you find yourself asking questions about human circumstances then the Anthropological Society then please consider coming to our monthly meetings.
summary: Anthropology is about human connections at its core. If you want to bring the humanity into any field you are studying or working in, please consider coming to our meetings where we will look into culture, language, archaeology, and human biology.

name: Pi Kappa Phi Fraternity
description: The Eta Chi Chapter of Pi Kappa Phi was first conceived during the summer of 1996. Four men on the New Student Orientation Staff had developed a unique friendship while working together throughout the summer. First, they had developed a special bond – a brotherhood – that they did not want to end with the summer. Second, many incoming freshmen with strong leadership backgrounds in high school were being drawn into fraternities that did not nurture their potential. In their opinion, fraternities at TCU fell into one category: drinking clubs. They felt it was a shame that many men like themselves who wanted a fraternal experience that was more than a “drinking club,” did not have a viable option within the existing Greek system. They also believed the TCU fraternities had strayed away from the original purpose of fraternity – scholarship, leadership, service, and social experiences. They took the necessary steps for fraternity expansion at TCU and in March 1998 the first members were officially initiated into Pi Kappa Phi Fraternity at TCU. Today, we keep that vision alive by maintaining the same values that the chapter was founded upon
summary: The Eta Chi Chapter of Pi Kappa Phi was first conceived during the summer of 1996. Four men on the New Student Orientation Staff had developed a unique friendship while working together throughout the summer. That is what brought Pi Kapp to TCU.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
description: The Iota Eta Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. was charted on the campus of Texas Christian University on April 22, 1972 by seven illustrious Delta Women. The Iota Eta chapter serves the TCU community through various empowerment sessions, public service opportunities, and socials. Delta Sigma Theta (ΔΣΘ) is a non-profit Greek-lettered sorority of college-educated women who perform public service and place emphasis on the African American community. Delta Sigma Theta was founded on January 13, 1913, at Howard University by twenty-two illustrious women and now, over 100 years later, Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Inc. has become the largest African-American Greek-lettered sorority in the world. Membership in Delta Sigma Theta is open to any woman who meets the membership requirements, regardless of race, nationality, or religion. Women may join through undergraduate chapters at a college or university, or after acquiring a college degree through a graduate chapter. The Grand Chapter has a membership of more than 350,000 predominantly African-American, college-educated women, and educated Caucasian, Asian, Native American, Hispanic, and African women. The sorority currently has 950-plus alumnae and collegiate chapters located in the United States, England, Japan (Tokyo and Okinawa), Germany, Bermuda, the Bahamas, Seoul, Saint Thomas and Saint Croix, U.S. Virgin Islands; Haiti and Jamaica.[1] Delta Sigma Theta is a member of multiple organizations, including the National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) — an organization of nine international Greek-letter sororities and fraternities - as well as the NAACP, and the National Council of Negro Women (NCNW). The current national president is Cheryl W Turner.

description:
Welcome to your new home in Milton Daniel Hall, home of the Dragons! We’re excited to have you as part of our vibrant and tight-knit community.

Built in 1957, Milton Daniel originally housed all male students and had interesting early mascots like the Monkeys, Miltonites, and the Mongols. name: TCU Frogs For Wildlife
description:
We are an official TCU student-led club, focused on the awareness, conservation, and advocation of all endangered animal species across the globe. We aim to engage with the TCU, and the larger Fort Worth, community in our efforts to support organizations actively making a difference. All while offering wonderful experiences for active TCU students interested in careers under the Ecology umbrella.

The TCU Rhino Initiative was started in April 2014, and our associated student organization, the Rhino Initiative Club, was created in 2019 to allow interested students to be a part of the solution and be involved in rhino conservation from here at TCU. This was then changed to the Frogs For Wildlife Club in December 2023 to include more endangered/threatened species.

We contribute through fundraising to support conservation efforts on the ground in South Africa. The annual Rhino Run 5K, aims to bring together a community of support for rhino conservation and raise money for the cause.

If you are interested in learning more about this organization or have questions, don't hesitate to reach out! You can email our President, Ava Delgado (ava.delgado@tcu.edu), or DM our Instagram: @tcufrogsforwildlife. Join the GroupMe for updates! https://groupme.com/join_group/54006440/uzpVQTsV
summary: Our purpose is to provide students with the opportunity to be actively involved in wildlife conservation. We aim to raise awareness and funds to help conserve species that are endangered or threatened.

name: Pre-Health Institute
description:
Please note: to officially be added as a TCU Pre-Health student, please reach out to Sarah Jung, Assistant Director for Pre-Health at sarah.jung@tcu.edu.

Please note: Pre-Health works with students on the following tracks: Pre-Med, Pre-Dent, Pre-PA, Pre-Optometry, Pre-Pharmacy, Pre-Podiatry, Pre-Genetic Counseling, Pre-Certified Anesthesiologist Assistant (CAA)

We do not work with Nursing, Pre-PT, or Pre-OT. Those tracks are served by the Harris College of Nursing and Health Sciences.

Since 1913 TCU has prepared students interested in the health professions for post-graduate study through rigorous coursework, access to high-quality clinical experiences and volunteer opportunities. We also provide personalized academic and pre-professional advising delivered by caring faculty mentors from the Biology, Chemistry & Biochemistry, Physics & Astronomy, Psychology & Neuroscience, and Nutrition Departments.

The mission of TCU’s Pre-Health Professions Institute is twofold:

Developing Skillful and Compassionate Future Healthcare Leaders: We are dedicated to equipping undergraduate students with the essential skills to emerge as competent, caring, and compassionate healthcare leaders.

Strategic Preparation for Professional School Admission: Through personalized advising and coursework, targeted programming, and rich professional development opportunities, we train our students to excel as standout candidates for admission to professional schools.

Our dual focus on character development and strategic preparation distinguishes TCU’s Pre-Health Professions Institute, making it a nurturing and empowering environment for the next generation of healthcare professionals.
summary: Please note: to officially be added as a TCU Pre-Health student, please reach out to Sarah Jung at sarah.jung@tcu.edu. The Pre-Health Professions Institute assists students interested in pursuing a career in a variety of health professions.

name: Students for a Dementia Friendly TCU
description:
Our organization's purpose is to raise awareness on TCU's campus about dementia and its impact on the individual, family, and community. We work diligently to change the social stigma that surrounds dementia and the diseases associated with it through advocacy, education, and community involvement. We have community representatives on our executive team who share their personal experiences with dementia and guide our team's efforts. Through Dementia Friendly Fort Worth, our organization offers unique volunteer opportunities and an opportunity to receive a Dementia Practitioner Certification. We are proud to be the only school in Texas with a Dementia Friendly affiliation. Everyday, we are actively working to end the stigma that surrounds dementia.

Join our movement to help CHANGE THE STIGMA! 

We are open to ALL TCU undergraduate and graduate students! 

Follow us on Instagram @dementiafriendlytcu and join our GroupMe for more updates (link in Instagram bio)! 

Questions? Email emma.rae.petty@tcu.edu
summary: Our purpose is to raise awareness about dementia and its impact on the individual, family, and community. As well as work to change the unfortunate social stigma that surrounds dementia through advocacy, education, and community involvement.

name: Narrative Medicine Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to analyze both fiction and non-fiction narratives to gain deeper insights into the medical field and the experiences of patients. We aim to deepen our understanding of patient perspectives, which will ultimately help us to become more compassionate and effective healthcare providers. Our discussions will focus on the themes present in the literature and the emotional or intellectual impacts of these readings. Through this exploration, we seek to bridge the gap between the health sciences and the humanities and strengthening connections among students within the Pre-Health Institute.
summary: We analyze fiction & non-fiction media about medical narratives to further our knowledge of the medical field and gain insight on the experiences and perspective of patients. We will bridge the gap between health sciences and the humanities.

name: Frogs 4 Children
description: Frogs 4 Children is a vibrant and compassionate student-led organization dedicated to serving children in our local community! We are comprised of enthusiastic and caring students of ALL majors. Our club offers a variety of impactful community service opportunities for the betterment of the children of our beloved Fort Worth community. Through engaging group activities, guest lectures, and mentoring, our club strives to equip our members with tools to support children from all kinds of backgrounds. Whether we are organizing educational workshops on campus, hosting fun-filled community service events for children, or partnering with Cook Children's hospital, Frogs 4 Children fosters a nurturing environment that promotes learning, professionalism, personal growth, and team building! Together, we are dedicated to brightening the futures of the children we serve and making a positive difference in our community. Join our GroupMe for announcements! https://web.groupme.com/join_group/95768089/6undcW3w
summary: An organization aimed at creating a community amongst students interested in helping children and making a difference in the community!

name: Her Campus at TCU
description:
Hi y'all! We're Her Campus at TCU, a chapter of a national organization that publishes articles by college women, for college women. We're here to unite students passionate about writing and female empowerment across all majors and to be an informative, uplifting voice on campus!

Our chapter writers publish their work on a blog hosted on HerCampus.com. We also host biweekly chapter meetings, writing workshops, empowerment events, and collaborations with TCU student organizations and local causes that align with our mission.

Find us on social media below:

INSTAGRAM             TWITTER/X             FACEBOOK            TIKTOK
summary: The purpose of this organization is to connect students across all majors who are passionate about writing and/or women’s empowerment to further their writing skills and create content for our Her Campus at TCU blog.

name: Chess Club
description:
Chess Club is a place for chess players to meet and have fun. In Chess Club we play chess games, talk about chess, and learn about chess. Don’t worry if you are new to chess because Chess Club is open to chess players of all skill levels!

Chess Club holds regularly scheduled meetings where we will discuss chess theory, play chess games, teach chess to new players, and talk about all things chess. We also host chess tournaments as a form of friendly competition!
summary: Chess Club is a place for chess players to meet and have fun. In Chess Club we play chess games, talk about chess, learn about chess, and host tournaments. Don’t worry if you are new to chess because we are open to chess players of all skill levels!

name: Impact of Words
description:
The purpose of Impact of Words is to spread small acts of kindness and aid students who feel depressed, anxious or isolated.  As an organization we meet once a week to write uplifting notes and pass them out to students, administration, and staff so that people feel welcomed on TCU's campus. We also plan big events like mental health week, raise money for mental health organizations, and partner with organization to make a true impact on campus. Our goal is to work towards solving the mental health issue that is so prevalent on college campuses. We also want to form an organization of fun, energized students who are excited about making other TCU students feel loved.

Impact of Words willbe able to offer student 1 hour of community service each weekly meeting. Our activities will consist of writing positive sticky notes, planning mental health week, educating TCU population on mental health, thank you cards and partnering with organization to spread awareness.
summary: Impact of Words spreads small acts of kindness and aids students who feel depressed, anxious or isolated.  As an organization we write uplifting notes and pass them out to students, administration, and staff so that people feel welcomed on TCU's campus.

name: Bingo in the Park
description:
The purpose of Bingo in the Park is to create empowering relationships between TCU students and people in the Fort Worth Area that are experiencing homelessness. Motivated by our Christian faith, we believe that relationships help restore the dignity of individuals experiencing homelessness.

Bingo in the Park will provide TCU students with the opportunity to learn about homelessness from a first-hand perspective and engage in the Fort Worth community.

If interested in joining us, please come to Oakland Lake Park Trailhead at 9 am on Friday mornings, and we will get you set up as a member!
summary: Bingo in the Park creates empowering relationships between TCU students and people who are experiencing homelessness in Fort Worth. Motivated by our faith, we believe relationships help restore the dignity of individuals experiencing homelessness.

name: Biology Club at TCU
description: The purpose of biology club is to provide an outlet for students to enjoy and develop a greater appreciation for Biology and related areas. Our club events include social events geared towards community building, volunteer opportunities, and panel events with professionals in the field. Involvement in biology club is a great opportunity to connect with other students that share your interests, as well as with the larger Fort Worth community.
summary: We provide an outlet for students to enjoy and develop a greater appreciation for Biology and related areas. Our club events include social events, volunteer opportunities, and panel events with professionals in the field.

name: TCU Twisters - Country Dance Club
description: The purpose of TCU Twisters is to promote love and knowledge of country dancing through learning, community, and fun. We teach partner dancing and line dancing, invite instructors to teach lessons, and provide fellowship among students. Come as you are. No partner? No boots? No experience? We can't wait to meet you. ♥︎
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote interest and knowledge of country dancing, teach partner dancing and line dancing, invite clinicians to teach lessons, and provide fellowship among students.

name: Patrick's Purpose to Value Life
description:
The goal of Patrick’s Purpose to Value Life is to create an authentic sense of awareness and cultivate action to prevent suicide by bringing light to a tragedy that hits close to home for many TCU students. We will work endlessly to save valuable and worthy lives by connecting students to resources. Additionally, Patrick’s Purpose will extensively reach out to educational resources and initiatives to educate the community on chronic depression.

Patrick’s Purpose will host seminars for guest speakers to share awareness on suicides, work jointly with outreach programs to honor individuals that have passed away due to suicide, and organize a suicide prevention walk.
summary: Our goal is to create an authentic sense of awareness and cultivate action to prevent adolescent suicides by bringing light to a tragedy that hits close to home for many TCU students. We will work endlessly to save valuable and worthy lives.

name: Frog Aides
description:
Frog Aides is a first-year student leadership opportunity (FLO) that equips students to become impactful leaders on campus and beyond.  

Past Frog Aides have gone on to lead in every aspect of the TCU community. Many Student Body Officers, SGA leaders, Greek Life leaders, Orientation Leaders, Frog Camp Facilitators, Connections Mentors, Tour Guides, campus mentors, and community leaders began their leadership development right here in Frog Aides. 

Applications and more information can be found on our website and our instagram (@tcufrogaides)!
summary: Frog Aides is a first-year student leadership opportunity that equips students to become impactful leaders on campus and beyond.

name: Neeley Office Of Inclusive Excellence
description: The TCU Neeley Office of Inclusive Excellence is a physical space for ALL Horned Frogs to gather & connect across differences.  The office is focused on creating and supporting a vibrant community that welcomes, embeds, respects and champions diverse cultures, identities and lived experiences. This office leads a summer bridge program for high school students to help cultivate interest in business careers and create pathways to enrollment.  Other access initiatives include: need based funding for professional attire and Microsoft Office Certification training and tests.
summary: The TCU Neeley Office of Inclusive Excellence is a physical space for ALL Horned Frogs to gather & connect across differences. The office is focused on creating and supporting a community that welcomes and champions diverse cultures and identities.

name: Student Success
description: The department of Student Success houses Orientation, Parent & Family Programs, tutoring, Student Success Coaching, Transfer Student Programs, and many other programs and initiatives that support students at TCU. We are located on the garden level of Samuelson Hall, west side entrance, past the post office.
summary: The department of Student Success houses Orientation, Parent & Family Programs, Peer2Peer Tutoring, Student Success Coaching, Transfer Student Programs, and many other programs and initiatives that support students at TCU.

name: TCU Flu Clinic
description: TCU's official Engage page for the TCU Flu Clinic on campus. The Flu Clinic will provide over 3,000 flu shots on October 6 to the campus community.
summary: TCU's official Engage page for the TCU Flu Clinic on campus.  The Flu Clinic will provide over 3,000 flu shots on October 6 to the campus community.

name: A Moment of Magic
description:
A Moment of Magic will provide vulnerable and underserved children with creative programming, outlets, and support to foster imagination, friendship, and hope through our college-student volunteers. We operate on the mission to improve the quality of life of vulnerable and underserved children and inspire them to be brave, strong, and fearless through fun and engaging social wellness activities.

 

If you are interested in creating some magic with us, join the GroupMe for information on upcoming meetings and events: https://groupme.com/join_group/109319616/oc9efShz
summary: A Moment of Magic will provide medically-vulnerable and underserved children with creative programming, outlets, and support to foster imagination, friendship, and hope through our college-student volunteers.

name: Ignite at TCU
description:
The purpose of IGNITE at TCU is to increase the political ambition, civic engagement, and leadership skills of its members in order to build the next generation of women leaders. As a non-partisan organization IGNITE at TCU:

•Provides its members with opportunities to meet and network with women political leaders.

•Provides its members with leadership and civic engagement opportunities.

•Provides a place of empowerment for women who are interested in learning about politics.

•Engages in civil discourse, while pursuing a vision where women across the political spectrum can work together to build each other’s political leadership.

•Supports young women to position themselves for candidacy.
summary: The purpose of Ignite at TCU is to increase the political ambition, civic engagement, and leadership skills of its members in order to build the next generation of women leaders.

name: Emergency Medical Services Club
description: The purpose of the TCU Emergency Medical Services Club is to help students obtain their emergency medical technician certification for use in ambulance and emergency room settings in order to serve citizens in the Fort Worth community and beyond. The TCU Emergency Medical Services Club will encourage students to obtain their emergency medical technician certification and work to find opportunities to gain patient care experience that will serve them for their future career in health care. This organization will also help foster a pre-health community on campus by uniting students based on their passion and interest in emergency medicine. Finally, this organization will work to educate the TCU community through tabling and outreach initiatives on common medical emergencies that they might face. The club will also serve as a foundation to work towards the peripheral goal of establishing an on-campus student-staffed EMS system at TCU.
summary: The TCU Emergency Medical Services Club encourages students to engage with the field of emergency medicine by providing information and pathways to obtaining an EMT license, connecting with local providers, and promoting public education first aid.

name: Harry Potter Club
description: The Harry Potter Club will provide TCU students with fun opportunities to form friendships while participating in Harry Potter-themed activities. These activities include but are not limited to team competitions, trivia nights, movie marathons, holiday events, and more.
summary: The purpose of the Harry Potter club is to foster friendships around the common interest of Harry Potter.

name: Co-Ed Running Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to create an inviting community that brings people together through their love for running. Additionally, the club will challenge members to grow personally and athletically as they train with each other and compete against others in local races and events.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to create an inviting community that brings people together through their love for running.

name: Women in Computer Science
description: The purpose of the Women in Computer Science organization is to support and empower female and non-binary undergraduate students. The organization aims to provide a community to encourage professional and personal development and to build a social, technical, and networking community for its members.
summary: The purpose of the Women in Computer Science organization is to support and empower female and non-binary undergraduate students in the Computer Science field by building a social, technical, and networking community for its members.

name: TCU Mock Trial
description: The TCU Mock Trial team competes against other colleges at American Mock Trial Association Tournaments. Students will play the roles of lawyers or witnesses for their team in front of judges. Members will gain public speaking, reading, writing, reasoning, and debate skills. Students will also become familiar with real courtroom procedures and will be exposed to the world of courtroom law. Members will gain a better understanding of what a career in law might be like.
summary: The purpose of TCU Mock Trial is to compete with other colleges in American Mock Trial Association Tournaments.

name: Nontraditional Frogs
description: Nontraditional Frogs exist for the purpose of creating a supportive community for nontraditional students. It is designed to provide the necessary support and resources to help meet the unique challenges of nontraditional students as they move through their educational experiences. To accomplish these goals, Nontraditional Frogs supports leadership training and events as well as provides a means for members to grow professionally and beyond the campus. Through our efforts and dedication, Nontraditional Frogs will bring a resilient and flourishing population to the diverse TCU culture.
summary: Nontraditional Frogs exist for the purpose of creating a supportive community for nontraditional students.

name: Bilingual Education Student Organization at TCU
description:
The Bilingual Education Student Organization is dedicated to creating an inclusive and supportive community for all bilingual and multilingual students, regardless of language background or proficiency level. We celebrate the rich diversity of cultures and languages within our campus through events, discussions, and initiatives that promote cross-cultural understanding and personal connection.

Our organization serves as a home away from home—an empowering space where students can share their unique linguistic and cultural identities while building lasting support networks both academically and personally.

A core part of our mission is educating the broader student body about the world’s languages—where they come from, how they’ve evolved, and the cultures they represent. Through interactive programming, cultural showcases, and educational outreach, we strive to raise awareness about the importance of linguistic diversity and the value of bilingualism in a global society.

By highlighting the origins and significance of different languages, we hope to inspire curiosity, appreciation, and respect for the many ways people communicate and connect across the world. Our goal is to amplify the voices of bilingual and multilingual students, advocate for inclusive practices in education, and foster a more informed, culturally aware, and globally minded campus community.
summary: The Bilingual Education Student Organization is dedicated to creating an inclusive and supportive community for all bilingual and multilingual students, regardless of language background or proficiency level.

name: Frogs for Pediatrics
description: The Frogs for Pediatric mission is to supply resources to patients and their family members in our local hospitals to help with a more comfortable hospital stay. We will also provide education to our members about the different fields of pediatrics through speakers and potential volunteer opportunities.
summary: The Frogs for Pediatric mission is to supply resources to patients and their family members in our local hospitals to help with a more comfortable hospital stay.

name: Cultural Connectors
description: TCU Cultural Connectors are student leaders in our residence halls who coordinate programs around Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion #Frogs4theCulture
summary: TCU Cultural Connectors are student leaders in our residence halls who coordinate programs around Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion #Frogs4theCulture

name: TCU Library
description: A place for research, studying, community lectures, library workshops, and events.
summary: A place for research, studying, community lectures, library workshops, and events.

name: FinTech Club
description:
The purpose of the FinTech club at Texas Christian University is to empower students of our community to act as leaders and ambassadors for the FinTech certificate by assisting younger students, discussing relevant research-based topics, and working with non-FinTech students to understand its role in their future.

Membership Requirements: 

Undergraduate students must be enrolled in a minimum of nine (9) credit hours per semester of membership.

 

What we plan to do: 

Host various panels, speaker events, tours, educational workshops, skills development, and mentorship programs for TCU students to develop professionally to learn and meet new people. 

 

Please join our GroupMe for all club related communications:

https://groupme.com/join_group/103992715/t4YxoX2o
summary: The purpose of this club is to educate individuals regarding Cryptocurrency, engage in discussion regarding market trends, crypto companies, and Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs).

name: M.O.V.E Texas Student Chapter
description: MOVE (Mobilize, Organize, Vote, Empower) Texas is a grassroots, nonpartisan, nonprofit organization building power in underrepresented youth communities through civic education, leadership development, and issue advocacy. We believe that together we are transforming democracy in Texas and beyond. The TCU chapter of MOVE Texas encourages voter registration, mobilization, and advocacy surrounding the issues students care about the most.
summary: The purpose of this club or organization shall be to educate and empower eligible youth voters.

name: CALM Meditation Group
description:
The purpose of the CALM Meditation Group is to provide a space and community for all TCU students to engage in meditation, contemplative thought, and mindfulness practices.

For more than a decade, TCU's CALM Studies initiative has been helping members of the TCU community and beyond reduce stress and anxiety, eating mindfully, foster belonging, and work to create a world that enables all beings to flourish.

We offer innovative courses, weekly meditations, vegetarian foods, lectures open to the community, as well as one-on-one meetings through our thriving CALM Convos and CALM Buddies programs.

CALM Mediation Group adheres to our four pillars of core values: belonging, compassion, wisdom, and flourishing.
summary: The purpose of the CALM Meditation Group is to provide a space and community for all TCU students to engage in meditation, contemplative thought, and mindfulness practices.

name: Korean Language and Culture Association
description: The Korean Language and Culture Association (KLCA) will provide monthly language sessions where members can learn the Korean language and practice their Korean skills with one another through fun, interactive activities. KLCA will also hold monthly events where members can learn about and engage with Korean culture. In addition, KLCA will host weekly K-Pop dance lessons through our branch organization, 3MILLION, where the TCU community can embrace Korean Pop culture.
summary: The purpose of KLCA is to promote the learning of the Korean language and to spread Korean culture with the TCU and Fort Worth community.

name: TCU Powerlifting
description:
Our Powerlifting Club is a welcoming, student-led organization focused on learning, strength, and personal growth through the sport of powerlifting. We provide a supportive environment for all experience levels, from beginners learning the basics to experienced lifters refining their technique. Members gain confidence in the gym, learn proper squat, bench press, and deadlift mechanics, and build discipline through consistent training. Beyond lifting, the club emphasizes community, encouragement, and accountability, creating a space where members can meet others, set goals, and celebrate progress. Competitive opportunities are available for those interested, but our main goal is to promote confidence, education, and a strong team culture.

Follow the Instagram:  @tcupowerlifiting   for more information!
summary: TCU Powerlifting is a club sport that welcomes all lifting experience and promotes strength, confidence, and powerlifting education, all within a supportive team environment that emphasizes community, growth, and meaningful relationships.

name: TCU Student Affairs
summary: This page is for use by the division of Student Affairs.

name: TCU LGBTQ+ Resources
description: LGBTQ+ Resources is located in the Intercultural Center, located on the first floor of the Brown-Lupton University Union (BLUU)! LGBTQ+ Resources is run by the Assistant Director, Joshua Mackrill (he/him) with the mission of supporting the LGBTQIA+ community at TCU through advocacy, education, and care. LGBTQ+ Resources puts on various trainings and workshops providing education around LGBTQIA+ topics ranging from a basic introductory workshop, Allies Training, or hosting programs related to current events. Everyone is welcome in the office, so please feel free to stop by or follow us on social media @tcu_lgbtq!
summary: TCU Engage page for LGBTQ+ Resources.

name: SIE Mentorship Program
description: The Purpose of this program is to equip students of color with the skills, knowledge, and connections to assist them in becoming inclusive and equitable leaders within the TCU community. The goal of the program is to promote student engagement and learning through a mentorship model that allows students to embrace the identities that they hold and to provide equitable opportunities and access to the students of color here at TCU.
summary: The official Engage page for the SIE Mentorship Program

name: Center for Supply Chain Innovation
description: The Center for Supply Chain Innovation is the nexus that brings together TCU students, world-class professors and business experts to improve global supply chains from end to end by developing talented professionals and fostering innovation.
summary: Engage page for the Center for Supply Chain Innovation within the Neeley School of Business

name: CHXX Who Lift
description: CHXX Who Lift empowers women in the weight room by talking about the techniques behind weight lifting, its benefits, as well as creating a positive mental environment where women can gather to discuss difficult topics such as mental health, body image, etc.  To create a stronger sense of community, all member meetings will be held twice a month. A "big/little" type program will be created between a returning lifter and a new lifter. Once the partnership is created, the returner and new lifter will be able to pick times during the week to go and lift! This new partnership will help women learn to lift weights and lift up each other mentally, socially and physically!
summary: CHXX Who Lift empowers women in the weight room, to learn more about weight lifting, and to foster a positive mental environment where women can gather to discuss difficult topics such as mental health, body image, etc.

name: Management Club
description:
The purpose of the Management Club is to provide events, networking opportunities, and resources for current and interested Neeley students. The Management Club aims to create a community of Neeley students seeking to gain more information and resources about a career in management. TCU Management Club gets plugged into DFW area businesses and TCU Neeley alumni.

 

Contact Sarai Morales to join!

469-990-0616
summary: The purpose of the Management Club is to provide events, networking opportunities, and resources for current and interested Neeley students interested in a career in management.

name: Minorities Opening Roads in Engineering
description: The purpose of M.O.R.E. is to challenge stereotypes in STEM and empower students in their college journey while giving back to the underrepresented communities these students came from. M.O.R.E. aims at fostering inclusivity within students of the TCU Engineering Department by fostering community, serving as a pipeline for nationwide engineering clubs (SWE, SHPE, ASME etc.) and increasing student retention per grade level. As  an organization, M.O.R.E will serve as a hub for students to communicate their academic, emotional, and social needs while in turn receiving help from resources within M.O.R.E. or outsourced from partnerships with other engineering clubs.
summary: The purpose of M.O.R.E. is to challenge stereotypes in STEM and empower students in their college journey while giving back to the underrepresented communities these students came from.

name: Sports Management Club
description:
The purpose of this organization is to provide students the opportunity to follow their dreams of working in one of the major American sports leagues – the NFL, MLB, NHL, and NBA. We focus on building connections, learning from professionals, getting involved, and networking.

Join our groupme for updates here: https://groupme.com/join_group/107423063/NaDI8sCv
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide students the opportunity to follow their dreams of working in one of the major American sports leagues – the NFL, MLB, NHL, and NBA.

name: Scribble Frogs
description:
As TCU's creative writing club, the purpose of Scribble Frogs is to create a safe, creative space at TCU for people of all majors to help them develop their unique voices, feel comfortable sharing their work, and have fun writing.

 

Scribble Frogs hosts weekly meetings and organizes guest speaker events, writing workshops, and writing competitions. Everyone is welcome, regardless of major!

 

Check out our website with past publications: https://www.scribblefrogstcu.com
summary: As TCU's creative writing club, Scribble Frogs strives to create a safe and collaborative space for student writers. During our weekly meetings, we have guest speakers, writing workshops, literary games, and more! Come to our meetings to learn more!

name: Japanese Language and Culture Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to bring awareness to and promote Japanese culture and language here on TCU’s campus in a fun and active manor. We strive to bring more emphasis of Japanese values to TCU’s campus and to give Japanese students and students affiliated with Japan a place to be with other like-minded individuals. Our goal is to create a community of students that will bring awareness to others and become more comfortable and understanding when working with others on a global scale.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to bring awareness to and promote Japanese culture and language here on TCU’s campus in a fun and active manor. We strive to bring more emphasis of Japanese values to TCU’s campus.

name: Royal Divas
description: The purpose of our majorette dance team is to continue to promote diversity on TCU's campus. It gives a sense of familiarity and home to African American women. it also supports the learning of a new culture on TCU's campus as well as showing individuality in a different dance form.
summary: The purpose of our majorette dance team is to continue to promote diversity on TCU's campus. It gives a sense of familiarity and home to African American women. it also supports the learning of a new culture on TCU's campus.

name: International Services
summary: International Services works to build innovative and tangible programs to engage international and domestic students towards TCU mission of creating responsible, ethical global leaders.

name: Kappa Delta
description: Kappa Delta Sorority is a national organization for women committed to: encouraging members to live with integrity and honor in the bonds of lifelong friendship; building confidence in members to excel in college and throughout their lives; inspiring members to be active leaders, responsible citizens and engaged members of the community; and providing opportunities and experiences that foster personal and professional growth for a lifetime.
summary: Kappa Delta Sorority provides experiences that build confidence in women and inspire them to action through the power of lifelong friendship.

name: Asian Pacific American Medical Student Association
description: The APAMSA Student Interest Group at the Burnett School of Medicine is organized with the purpose of providing a space for both Asian and non-Asian students to promote awareness and advocacy of issues affecting AANHPI communities.
summary: The APAMSA Student Interest Group at the Burnett School of Medicine is organized with the purpose of providing a space for both Asian and non-Asian students to promote awareness and advocacy of issues affecting AANHPI communities.

name: Rural, International, & Global Health
description:
Activities:

• Standardized patients to practice cultural sensitivity in the healthcare setting

• Speakers/seminar series

• Book club

• Supply procurement

• Ethics conversations (about working abroad)

• Partnering with TCU Undergrad

• International rotations (India, South Africa, Taiwan, etc)

• Rural Health volunteering opportunities in Texas and nearby states

• Mission trips

• Global health research

• Journal clubs (ex. Case studies)

• Volunteering at Dr. Bonnell’s clinics
summary: The RIG SIG seeks to address issues regarding healthcare disparities globally and rurally while also uniting, educating, and inspiring students to pursue health equity.

name: American Association of Neurological Surgeons
description: Our AANS chapter supports students interested in exploring neurosurgery by facilitating exposure to the field and building relationships between students, faculty, and residents.
summary: TCU Burnett School of Medicine's American Association of Neurological Surgeons Student Interest Group

name: American Medical Women's Association
summary: AMWA supports women in medicine and engages with women in our community. Here we lift up each other and our community.

name: Anesthesiology Interest Group
summary: The Anesthesiology Interest Group (AIG) will expose students early and often to the field of anesthesiology by connecting with fellow students, faculty, and physicians who are passionate and committed to the field.

name: Association of Women Surgeons
summary: AWS

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: The purpose of this organization is to empower the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development.

name: Leadership and Academics for STEM Students Organization
description: An Academic and Leadership Organization on TCU's campus that is committed to empowering and supporting STEM students from all lifestyles and backgrounds on each of their individual journeys through the field
summary: This organization would serve as a community and network building tool for STEM students. We focus on engagement with other students in similar fields of study, while providing mentorship and opportunities for service.

name: OneShadeGreener
description: At OneShadeGreener, our commitment to creating a cleaner, greener, and more sustainable world is reflected in our community-centered, action-oriented approach. As a non-profit environmental organization, we believe that every individual has the power to make a positive impact on the planet and in their community. We focus on three key pillars: Community Clean-Ups, Simple Sustainable Living, and Combating Food Insecurity.
summary: Our dedication lies in taking tangible action toward fostering a cleaner environment. Through community-driven clean-up initiatives, educational and impactful projects, we aim to create a positive and lasting impact on our environment and community.

name: TCU NRF Student Association
description:
TCU Chapter of NRF Student Association (Formerly Fashion Forward)

*PURPOSE: The purpose of the TCU Chapter of the NRF Student Association (formerly Fashion Forward) is to create an atmosphere that allows for network expansion, fosters creativity, and generates curiosity and interest about the fashion industry.

*FUTURE PLANS: guest speakers and networking events, volunteer opportunities and community engagement, tour retail stores or production facilities, networking with alumni, attend local fashion industry events, etc.
summary: The purpose of the TCU Chapter of the NRF Student Association is to create an atmosphere that allows for network expansion, fosters creativity, and generates curiosity and interest about the fashion industry.

name: National Society of Leadership and Success
description:
The NSLS is an organization that provides a life-changing leadership program that helps members achieve personal growth, career success, and empowers them to positively impact their communities. We help you identify career goals, hone leadership skills, turn ambitions into action, and provide the essential tools to help you excel in your future and make a change in the world.

As a member of the NSLS, you're part of a community of goal-oriented people with access to professional leadership training, exclusive scholarships, an online job bank, networking events, Speaker Broadcasts, enhancement of your professional resume, and more.

WE BELIEVE THAT:

- Learning is a life-long journey.

- Everyone has leadership potential.

- We achieve more together than we do alone.
summary: The mission of the NSLS is to help people discover and achieve their goals. It offers life-changing lectures from the nation's leading presenters and communities where like-minded, success-oriented individuals come together and help one another succeed.

name: TCU PACK
description: Help raise money for kids with cancer and parents who need help with other bills, due to their child's cancer. Create letters for kids in the hospitals and host events in-person and virtual to raise more awareness.
summary: The mission of TCU PACK is to help kids with cancer and their families by providing financial and emotional support.

summary: HEENT-Plastic Surgery

name: Pediatric Student Interest Group
summary: Pediatric Student Interest Group

name: Latino Medical Student Association
description: Latino Medical Student Association (LMSA) is an organization that has nationwide participation. The organization strives to unite and empower current and future physicians through service, mentorship, and education to advocate for the improved health of Hispanic and Latina/o/x community in the United States. At TCU, our chapter is one of many across the country supporting the mission of LMSA.
summary: Latino Medical Student Association

name: Dermatology Interest Group
description: The Dermatology Special Interest Group for Medical Students provides a dynamic forum for diving into the world of dermatology. Through workshops, clinical experiences, research opportunities, and networking events, we aim to deepen understanding and enthusiasm for skin health. Whether you're an avid dermatology enthusiast or new to the field, all are welcome to join us in exploring the fascinating realm of dermatology.
summary: Dermatology Interest Group

name: Jewish Student Interest Group

name: Cardiology Interest Group
description:
The Cardiology Interest Group (CIG) at the Burnett School of Medicine at TCU is dedicated to promoting a comprehensive understanding of cardiology through a variety of enriching activities. Our goal is to provide a dynamic platform where students can engage deeply with the cardiological sciences and build a strong foundation for their future careers.

Our activities include regular meetings with esteemed faculty advisors and board members, creating a robust network for guidance and learning. We host distinguished guest speakers ranging from residents to attendings, offering members invaluable insights and firsthand accounts of the cardiology field. A monthly journal club encourages members to delve into current research topics, fostering critical thinking and discussion amongst peers.

Additionally, we arrange immersive clinical experiences, allowing members to witness cardiology in action and apply their knowledge in real-world settings. Our members also have the chance to attend national cardiology conferences, connecting with the broader medical community and staying abreast of the latest developments in cardiology.

Community engagement is another cornerstone of our group, with numerous volunteer opportunities that underscore the importance of service and impact. Moreover, we actively seek collaboration with other student interest groups to broaden our perspectives and understand the interdisciplinary nature of healthcare.

In summary, our group is not just about learning cardiology but also about igniting a lifelong passion for the specialty through mentorship, education, and hands-on experiences.
summary: Explore the multifaceted field of cardiology! CIG champions mentorship, immersive clinical experiences, and inspiring talks from experts, providing hands-on opportunities that cultivate a deep passion and comprehensive understanding of cardiology.
