name: Student Interest Group in Neurology
description:
Welcome to the TCU Medical School Neurology Interest Group! Our organization is dedicated to fostering a strong connection between medical students interested in neurology and the vibrant neurology community in the Dallas-Fort Worth (DFW) area. We aim to provide opportunities for students to engage with patients across various neurological spectrums, including critical care, mental health, and chronic pathologies, while also facilitating learning through simulation events and collaborative educational initiatives.

Mission Statement:
Our mission is to empower future neurologists by:

1. Connecting with the DFW Neurology Community: We provide avenues for students to interact with local neurologists, researchers, and healthcare professionals through networking events, guest lectures, and clinical shadowing opportunities. These connections offer valuable insights into the field of neurology and potential career paths.

2. Volunteering with Patients: Our group is committed to making a positive impact on the lives of patients with neurological conditions. Through volunteer initiatives at critical care units, mental health facilities, and clinics serving patients with chronic pathologies, members gain firsthand experience in patient care, empathy, and advocacy.

3. Educational Opportunities: We organize simulation events, workshops, and seminars led by experts in neurology to enhance students' understanding of neurological disorders, diagnostic techniques, treatment modalities, and recent advancements in the field. Collaborative learning environments foster critical thinking, problem-solving skills, and interdisciplinary teamwork.

Membership Benefits:
Joining our Neurology Interest Group offers numerous benefits, including:

- Networking opportunities with neurology professionals
- Hands-on experience through patient volunteering
- Access to educational resources and workshops
- Mentorship opportunities with faculty and resident physicians
- Involvement in community outreach initiatives
- Preparation for residency applications and future career paths in neurology

Get Involved:
Whether you are a seasoned neurology enthusiast or simply curious about the field, we welcome all TCU Medical School students who share a passion for neuroscience and patient-centered care. By joining our group, you'll have the chance to expand your knowledge, build meaningful connections, and contribute to the well-being of individuals living with neurological disorders.

Connect with us to learn more about upcoming events and how you can get involved with the TCU Medical School Neurology Interest Group. Let's explore the fascinating world of neurology together!
summary: Student Interest Group in Neurology

FIND THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS. PERSONAL AND PROFESSIONAL.

Our holistic approach aims to create an all-encompassing experience that supports students in multiple facets of their personal and professional growth. Our chapter aspires to build others up. It is not about what you've done, but how you aspire to continue growing.

Our goal is to provide insight into the roles and responsibilities of a nurse anesthetist, while also fostering connections between TCU’s CRNA program and our undergraduate BSN students. As a member, you'll gain valuable information about the profession, have opportunities to ask questions, and receive support in becoming a competitive applicant for graduate school.

If you're interested, we’d love for you to request membership through TCU Engage and join us at our monthly meetings. Can’t wait to meet you soon!

name: Young Americans for Freedom Chapter
description:
Who are we? A national student organization that stands for Conservatism and traditional American values at TCU.

 

In this time of moral and political crises, it is the responsibility of the youth of America to affirm certain eternal truths.

 

We, as young conservatives, believe: That foremost among the transcendent values is the individual’s use of his God-given free will, whence derives his right to be free from the restrictions of arbitrary force; That liberty is indivisible, and that political freedom cannot long exist without economic freedom; That the purpose of government is to protect those freedoms through the preservation of internal order, the provision of national defense, and the administration of justice; That when government ventures beyond these rightful functions, it accumulates power, which tends to diminish order and liberty; That the Constitution of the United States is the best arrangement yet devised for empowering government to fulfill its proper role, while restraining it from the concentration and abuse of power; That the genius of the Constitution—the division of powers—is summed up in the clause that reserves primacy to the several states, or to the people, in those spheres not specifically delegated to the Federal government; That the market economy, allocating resources by the free play of supply and demand, is the single economic system compatible with the requirements of personal freedom and constitutional government, and that it is at the same time the most productive supplier of human needs; That when government interferes with the work of the market economy, it tends to reduce the moral and physical strength of the nation; that when it takes from one man to bestow on another, it diminishes the incentive of the first, the integrity of the second, and the moral autonomy of both; That we will be free only so long as the national sovereignty of the United States is secure; that history shows periods of freedom are rare, and can exist only when free citizens concertedly defend their rights against all enemies; That the forces of international Communism are, at present, the greatest single threat to these liberties; That the United States should stress victory over, rather than coexistence with, this menace; and That American foreign policy must be judged by this criterion: does it serve the just interests of the United States?
summary: Our purpose is to defend Conservative principles at TCU. We stand for individual freedom, free enterprise, a strong national defense, and traditional American values.

summary: Leaders Engaging & Assisting People through Service (LEAPS) is a First-Year Leadership Organization (FLO) that focuses on community engagement and servant leadership, while also developing and executing TCU Serves Days, etc.

name: Iranian American Medical Association
summary: To unite the Iranian student doctors at the Anne Burnett Marion School of Medicine at Texas Christian University in order to foster community, offer opportunities for mentorship and connections, and further, to uplift the Iranian students.

name: Fishers of Men
description: We will host pond clean-ups with other TCU organization, we will fish around Ft. Worth, have a bible studies, and serve our community through service projects.  It is open to any undergraduate or graduate student at Texas Christian University, regardless of class year or major.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide opportunities for all current college students to belong to a community built on faith, service, and conservation.

name: Active Minds
description:
Active Minds will host community building events, speakers, discussions, stress relief
activities, and tabling events. Active Minds is open to any undergraduate or graduate student at Texas Christian University, regardless of class year or major.
summary:
The purpose of this organization is to promote mental health awareness and education for young adults, and encourage them to be authentic, empowering to the community and to
pursue smart, bold innovation.

name: Black Musicans Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide Black and African American students who are interested in the field of music a space to collaborate, build community, and learn more about the realities of having a career in music as a Black/African American person. BMA will host performance opportunities, lecturers, workshops for various musical career paths, fundraising events, and other events that outreach to the nearby music community. BMA is open to all students who attend Texas Christian University, including non-music majors. However, the organization is designed for students who intend to pursue a music career, whether it be in education, performance, composition, etc.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide Black and African American students who are interested in the field of music a space to collaborate, build community, and learn more about the realities of having a career in music.

name: Clinical Research Society
description:
Summary of the Clinical Research Society (CRS)

The Clinical Research Society (CRS) at Texas Christian University (TCU) is committed to fostering interest in clinical and scientific research among both medical and undergraduate students. Its mission focuses on equipping members with foundational research knowledge and skills, and cultivating a robust network among students and faculty. Grounded in ethical leadership, responsible citizenship, and collaboration, the CRS aligns closely with TCU’s commitment to integrity, community, and excellence.

 

Key Objectives and Activities:

  1. Research Exposure: Provide seminars on diverse topics that spark student interest.
  2. Collaboration: Building partnerships with TCU faculty, medical professionals, and fellow students.
  3. Events: Host an annual CRS Research Symposium to highlight student projects and foster networking, alongside a speaker series and workshops.
summary: Inspire and support students with an interest in clinical or scientific research by helping them develop the knowledge, skills, and passion necessary for success in the field.

name: Opera Student Society
description: Join to connect with fellow opera enthusiasts, expand your skills, and engage in performances. Our organization aims to promote opera within the community while fostering a supportive environment for all members. The Opera Student Society is open to all TCU students, undergraduate and graduate, regardless of class year and major. Become a part of our vibrant community!
summary: The purpose of Opera Student Society is to unify students who share an interest in opera to create connections, expand skills, encourage involvement in campus performances, and promote opera to the community.

name: Diamond Crew
description: Diamond Crew supports and promotes TCU Baseball, one of the most prestigious college baseball programs in the country, by collaborating with the TCU Athletics and TCU Marketing departments to lead game day activities on campus, like giveaways, tablings, and in-game experiences. Whether you’re a passionate baseball fan, wanting to learn more about the university and sport, or desiring to join a new community, Diamond Crew will knock it out of the park!
summary: TCU Diamond Crew is an organization whose mission is to support and promote TCU Baseball, will collaborate with the TCU Marketing and TCU Athletics Departments, and will consist of a group of students who are fully committed to the program and values

name: Orthodox Christian Campus Ministries
description: OCCM anchors college students to the values and ancient faith of the Orthodox Christian Church through fellowship, prayer, service to others, and study of the faith. Christ’s final command is to “go therefore and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all things that I have commanded you” (Matthew 28:19-20). This command is for all Christians, not just the Apostles. It is our duty as Christians to make sure the doors are visible and open to all. In other words, each of us is to welcome inquirers of the Orthodox Christian Faith with a warm smile and get to know them. Through these small acts of love, we share Orthodoxy with others and become a beacon of light on campus.
summary: OCCM serves to unite Coptic Orthodox Christian students from different college campuses in Coptic Orthodox worship and fellowship.

name: Disc Golf
description:
The TCU Disc Golf Club brings students together to learn, play, and have fun with one of the fastest-growing sports out there. Our goal is simple: create a welcoming space where anyone can pick up a disc, enjoy the outdoors, and make new friends along the way.

We keep it laid-back with fun monthly meetings on campus and casual outings to local courses. Whether you’re a first-timer or an experienced player, you’ll find a community that’s all about staying active, connecting with others, and having a good time.

Follow us on Instagram @tcudiscgolf to stay up to date with events and course days!
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide opportunities for Disc Golf enthusiasts to have a community here at TCU.

name: Freedom Frogs
description: TCU students actively contribute to supporting those who have served our nation by engaging with the nearly 44,000 veterans in Fort Worth. Many TCU students come from military families, which fosters a deep sense of empathy for the challenges these veterans face. Through volunteering, students not only provide assistance but also align with TCU's mission to develop ethical leaders and responsible citizens who contribute to the global community.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide philanthropic and fundraising services for local veterans.

name: TCU Rocks
description: When you come to one of our meetings you will be greeted with a smile and given the time to let your creativity run free. We want all of us to come together and have rock painting parties at least once a month! Our rocks will be shared all around campus in hopes to brighten someone's day. Paint your rocks with quotes, uplifting sayings, cute images, or anything you'd like! WE LOVE YOU!
summary: Our purpose is to bring students together in a safe space to promote kindness all around campus and in our community while letting our members freely express their creativity through rock painting.

name: Illuminate
description: Illuminate exists to raise awareness and mobilize resources to translate the Bible and help eradicate Bible poverty. Through our partnership with IllumiNations, we organize fundraising and service initiatives that support global Bible translation efforts. Guided by Matthew 28:19 and Revelation 7:9–10, we seek to see God’s Word accessible to everyone.
summary: Illuminate aims to connect TCU and greater community to the mission of eradicating Bible poverty!

name: UKirk TCU
summary: This organization is called to be a community of those who want to learn about and follow Jesus Christ in our common life and reach out in love to our neighbors, while seeking justice, loving kindness and walking humbly with God.

name: Women in Sports Network
description: A student organization dedicated to fostering connections and providing support among women looking to work in the sports industry!
summary: The purpose of this organization is dedicated to fostering and building connections and providing support among women looking to work in the sports industry.

name: Black Graduate Business Association
description:
The Black Graduate Business Association (BGBA) is committed to fostering a strong sense of community among black graduate students by providing opportunities for networking, mentorship, professional development, and social engagement. We advocate for the advancement of our members, working to ensure their success within the university and beyond.

 

Additionally, BGBA is dedicated to creating a safe and supportive space where issues related to diversity, equity, and inclusion can be openly discussed and addressed, both within the university and in the broader community.
summary: The mission of the BGBA is to foster an inclusive community for Black graduate students in business, promote professional development, and advocate for diversity and equity within the business school. The BGBA aims to connect students, mentors, alumni.

name: Mu Delta
description:
Welcome to Mu Delta, a professional pre-health fraternity! Our mission is to build a supportive community that connects you with professionals in the fields you’re passionate about. We will host meetings each semester where doctors, surgeons, nurses and other healthcare professionals share their stories, career paths, and valuable insights into their journeys. Through these connections, you’ll have the opportunity to ask questions and seek guidance on any challenges you may face along the way. Stay tuned for updates, and don’t hesitate to reach out with any questions!

 

 

 

Mu Delta Goals 

1. Direct Access to Healthcare Professionals - Provide members with exclusive opportunities to engage with doctors, surgeons, nurses, and other professionals who share real-world experiences, offer guidance, and help shape future career paths.

2.Personalized Mentorship & Guidance - Members can seek mentors who can offer one-on-one advice tailored to their specific healthcare career interests, ensuring individualized support beyond general networking.

3.Ethical Leadership in Healthcare - Encourage members to engage in discussions and initiatives surrounding medical ethics, healthcare policy, and patient advocacy to develop compassionate, socially responsible healthcare leaders.

4.Collaboration & Inclusivity - Foster a welcoming environment where all pre-health students can share knowledge, support one another, and grow together.

5. Pre-Health & Nursing Student Retention - Exposure to the real-life experiences of healthcare professionals provides students with a broader perspective on their career journey, emphasizing resilience, perseverance, and personal growth beyond academic performance and other factors.

 

Join our Group me: https://groupme.com/join_group/104188435/BxSYfMGy
summary: Create an environment that allows students interested in health careers to be accepted and heard and offer discussions and speeches by healthcare professionals to offer their expertise and advice about their professions.

name: LGBTQ Christian SIG
summary: n/a

name: Red Cross Club
description: The TCU Red Cross Club is affiliated with the National Red Cross and works to build upon their mission statement, which is to prevent and alleviate human suffering in the face of emergencies by mobilizing the power of volunteers and the generosity of donors.​ We do this by hosting meetings and conducting events which align with their 5 main scopes of service which are Disaster Relief, Lifesaving Blood, Training Courses and Certification, Military Families, and International Services.
summary:
The purpose of this organization is to provide opportunities for those interested in service and involvement,
specifically in global and local emergencies.

name: Dance and Medicine SIG
description:
The purpose of this student interest group is to be a space for all that
are interested in the overlap between Dance, the art, and the practice
of Medicine. This SIG’s mission is to inform the future physicians at
Burnett how to best care for their patient dancers, to learn preventive
and rehabilitative exercises, and above all, to create a positive
atmosphere for dancers to come together to have fun.
summary: The Dance and Medicine SIG desires to be a space for all that are interested in the overlap between Dance, the art, and the practice of Medicine.

Your hands-on experience may range from planning and enhancing existing traditions, to implementing your new ideas, all while creating long-lasting friendships with other leaders on campus.
summary: A student-led, departmentally supported program dedicated to enhancing Spirit and Traditions at TCU.

name: Advances in Preventive Nutrition
description:
The purpose of this organization is to bridge a gap in healthcare training. Advances in Prevention Nutrition provides a platform for students to learn about the critical intersection of nutrition, physiology, and disease prevention— giving them a comprehensive understanding of how lifestyle choices directly impact health.

name: American Medical Women's Association - TCU Undergraduate Chapter
description:
Advancing women in medicine for a thriving, equitable world. 

This organization will work to bring together women pre-health students; to promote their education and training; to educate them and the public on mental and physical health issues; to encourage women to study and practice medicine; to ensure them equal opportunity to do so; to work to further its objectives and to support medical relief services; and to cooperate with other organizations having comparable interests.

name: Bags of Joy
description: The purpose of this organization is to bring joy and create meaningful connections with underserved elementary school students in our community through fun, engaging, and thoughtful events. By spreading kindness through acts of generosity like candy bags, arts and crafts, and more, we aim to inspire a sense of belonging and brighten the lives of the children we serve. We believe that small gestures can have a big impact, and we are committed to fostering a spirit of community and care.
summary: Bags of Joy

name: Hands of Hope
description:
A fun way to get involved in philanthropy with your friends! Come, hang out, make bracelets that will be sent to orphans around the world. Time commitment can be as much or as little as you please. Bracelets will be sent to orphans that are victims of abuse and trafficking. Partnered with the charity Orphaned Starfish Foundation to promote positivity and love around the world!

summary: Hands of Hope

name: Honorable Men of TCU
description:
Honorable Men of TCU is a brotherhood of driven, principled, and culturally aware men whose values are brotherhood, scholarship, cultural awareness, and community service. We are men committed to excellence, leadership, and uplifting one another while making a lasting impact on our campus and community.

Our organization serves as a foundation and steppingstone toward bringing a Fraternity to Texas Christian University. We strive to embody honor, integrity, and unity in everything we do, fostering a space where men of all backgrounds can grow personally, academically, and professionally.

We believe in building leaders who serve with purpose, celebrating diversity, and creating a legacy rooted in respect and empowerment. Those who join Honorable Men of TCU are not just joining an organization they’re becoming part of a movement grounded in culture, brotherhood, and excellence.
summary: The Honorable Men of TCU is a passionate and driven organization at Texas Christian University.

name: Leukemia and Lymphoma Society at TCU
description: The mission of LLS at TCU is to provide a means for students at TCU to meet and fulfill the mission of LLS. We hope to build leaders on campus who are passionate about raising awareness for cancer. Being part of an LLS chapter would allow students to gain leadership experience, develop project management skills, connect with industry professionals, and engage in meaningful philanthropic work that will stay with them for a lifetime.
summary: The official mission of our national affiliate, The Leukemia & Lymphoma Society (LLS) is “to cure leukemia, lymphoma, Hodgkin’s disease and myeloma, and improve the quality of life of patients and their families.”

name: Riff Ram Readers
description: Riff Ram Readers is Texas Christian University’s first book club, dedicated to creating a welcoming space where students can engage with diverse literature and connect through meaningful discussions. By exploring various genres and perspectives in a student-led, inclusive environment, the organization aims to broaden understanding and contribute to TCU’s mission of developing ethical leaders and responsible citizens. The club will host biweekly book discussions, where members can come together to discuss the assigned book for the semester. In addition, Riff Ram Readers will organize other events like author talks and community outreach programs to promote literacy and encourage engagement with literature. These initiatives will cultivate thoughtful conversations, expose students to different viewpoints, and strengthen the sense of community among readers at TCU.
summary: Riff Ram Readers will provide TCU students with a space to engage with diverse literature, connect through meaningful discussions, and foster a shared love for reading.

name: Society of Automotive Engineers
description: The Society of Automotive Engineers is a global organization dedicated to advancing mobility engineering and innovation. Here at TCU, our SAE chapter works to encourage students to explore the automotive industry and provides avenues for hands-on learning and development. By fostering connections with industry partners, we aim to bring valuable networking and internship opportunities to campus. In addition to strengthening these ties, our chapter aims to competes in collegiate engineering contests against other schools to promote TCU’s growing engineering department and highlight our talent on a larger stage.
summary: Society of automotive engineers

name: Students Consulting for Non Profits
description: The TCU chapter of Student Consulting for Nonprofit Organizations (SCNO) is a new member of a growing national network of top university students committed to enriching and uplifting the global nonprofit community through pro-bono consulting. Our mission is rooted in three pillars: empowering communities, developing leaders, and delivering professional results. By applying the diverse skill sets and academic backgrounds of our members, we aim to drive tangible, lasting impact for nonprofit organizations in Fort Worth and beyond. SCNO provides TCU students with the opportunity to engage in real-world projects that strengthen both qualitative and quantitative abilities, while deepening their understanding of social issues. Each project team will include associate consultants, a project manager, and a senior advisor—working together to deliver high-quality, actionable solutions that help our nonprofit partners further their missions. As we build this chapter, we’re committed to developing empathetic, capable leaders and creating meaningful change in the community we serve.
summary: Students Consulting for Non-Profit Organizations

name: TCU Global Ambassadors
description:
This is a unique opportunity for students to continue their international education journey after study
abroad, all while giving back to the TCU Community by sharing their own intercultural experiences. We
invite you to promote study abroad programs to students who will follow in your footsteps. Help others
become knowledgeable of the education abroad opportunities at TCU Global and encourage students to
take those next steps in going global! You will be a part of panels, study abroad fairs, intercultural events,
bonding events and so much more!
summary: TCU Global Ambassadors

name: TCU Health and Fitness
description:
TCU Health and Fitness is a community based organization that focuses on encouraging and educating fellow students throughout their health and fitness journeys. The organization hosts meetings, seminars, and physical activity events frequently to achieve such goals. 

Follow us on Instagram: @tcu.healthandfitness
summary: TCU Health and Fitness

name: TCU Hip Hop
description: The purpose of our organization is to provide opportunities for students at TCU with a passion for dance a non-competitive setting focused on the style of hip hop, where they can strive for excellence, grow and enhance their skills, build a sense of community, and actively engage with their peers. We are looking for students at TCU who have some sort of dance experience. Our meetings will be in a dance class style where we start with a warmup, learn a dance, and then we will perform it for each other. We would love to have a few members who are willing to choreograph for a class as well.
summary: TCU Hip Hop

name: Texas Christian University Chapter of Ducks Unlimited
description:
Our Ducks Unlimited Chapter at TCU will host its first event next fall (date and location TBD). The event will feature an auction and raffle, with all proceeds benefiting Ducks Unlimited.

If you're interested in joining our chapter, we hold meetings on the first Monday of every month. For more information or questions, please contact:

  • Gavin McLiney: 913-669-3894

  • Whitt Horton: 817-404-7273
summary: Ducks Unlimited

name: ASL Engagement
description: American Sign Language Engagment is an organization that prioritizes students knowledge of Deaf Culture! This organization aims to provide the opportunity for students to bridge communication gaps, promote and foster an inclusive community, and practice their American Sign Language. Additionally we strive to promote awareness about the Deaf community and interact through philanthropic events and other activities!
summary: ASL Engagement

name: Transaction and Investment Professionals Board
description:
We put the power of successful TCU alumni in the finance field to work for you. Our Transaction and Investment Professionals (TIP) Board will guide you through résumé writing, interview preparation, internship and career opportunities, and career planning.

Since the founding of the Transaction and Investment Professionals Board in 2014, the average annual student placement has tripled.

Mission: To promote the competitiveness and placement of TCU students and alumni among the highest ranks of the transaction and related industries including investment banking, equity research, private equity and hedge funds.

Vision: To build a successful and sustainable organization through the development and mentorship of students, giving them the resources and support to develop into confident and inspiring professionals.
summary: TIP Board aims to build a successful and sustainable organization through the development and mentorship of students, giving them the resources and support to develop into confident and inspiring professionals.

name: FAITHful Frogs
description: FAITHfulFrogs is a First-Year Leadership Opportunity (FLO) that focuses on faith development, spiritual wellness, and interfaith engagement.  First-Year students in this FLO will learn more about TCU’s religious identity, develop self-awareness of what they believe & why they believe it, explore ways to intentionally grow in their faith & be spiritually well while at TCU, and learn how to live in community with people from other religious traditions and faith identities.  At the conclusion of their academic year, FAITHful Frog members will create and present to their cohort how they plan to contribute to TCU’s religious & spiritual life moving forward.
summary: FAITHfulFrogs is a First-Year Leadership Opportunity (FLO) that focuses on faith development, spiritual wellness, and interfaith engagement.

name: Frog PATH
description: Frog PATH is a First-Year Leadership Opportunity (FLO) that provides a community for first-year students passionate about identity, connection, and transformational growth. Designed for students from multifaceted backgrounds, Frog PATH empowers you to explore who you are, build lifelong friendships, and become a leader on campus and beyond. Through workshops, mentorship, and campus resources, you’ll find a sense of belonging and the tools to thrive at TCU.
summary: Frog PATH is a First-Year Leadership Opportunity (FLO) that provides a community for first-year students passionate about identity, connection, and transformational growth.

name: Lead OUT Loud
description: Lead OUT Loud is a vibrant and empowering First-Year Leadership Opportunity (FLO) designed for first-year college students at TCU who are interested in learning more about the LGBTQ+ Community or allies. This program fosters a welcoming space to build meaningful connections, develop leadership skills, explore identity, and contribute to creating a more inclusive and affirming campus environment.
summary: Lead OUT Loud is a vibrant and empowering First-Year Leadership Opportunity (FLO) designed for first-year college students at TCU who are interested in learning more about the LGBTQ+ Community or allies.

name: The Leap
description: The Leap is TCU 360's weekly pop culture show, created and produced by TCU students.
summary: The Leap is TCU 360's weekly pop culture show, created and produced by TCU students.

name: Unscripted
description: Unscripted is TCU 360's weekly sports talk show, following sports at TCU, in Texas and across the nation.
summary: Unscripted is TCU 360's weekly sports talk show, following sports at TCU, in Texas and across the nation.

name: Center for Writing
description: The William L. Adams Center for Writing at TCU provides comprehensive writing support for students at all stages of the writing process. We offer a wide range of services, including in-person and Zoom appointments, online submissions, and writing groups, to assist with everything from academic essays and research papers to creative writing and job portfolios.
summary: The William L. Adams Center for Writing at TCU provides comprehensive writing support for students at all stages of the writing process. We offer a wide range of services, including in-person and Zoom appointments, online submissions, and writing groups.

name: Nationally Competitive Scholarships
description:
Nationally Competitive Scholarships (NCS) helps TCU students discover and apply for prestigious, external awards to study, research, and lead around the world. While these awards do not typically fund TCU tuition, they enhance your degree—broadening your learning, expanding your network, and setting you apart. NCS offers info sessions about different awards, writing workshops to refine your application materials, and long-term planning and support to help you develop the experiences that make you a strong candidate.

NCS is housed in the Honors College but open to all students!
summary: Nationally Competitive Scholarships (NCS) helps TCU students discover and apply for prestigious, external awards to study, research, and lead around the world.

name: Jesus Loves You
description: The purpose of Jesus Loves You is to provide a welcoming, student-led environment where faith feels personal, honest, and approachable. We recognize that speaking in front of pastors or religious leaders can sometimes feel intimidating, so our community removes that barrier, allowing students to connect, reflect, and learn together in a space built on mutual respect and love. Our goal is simple: no judgment, no hierarchy, just honest conversations that help each of us grow in faith and remind one another that Jesus truly loves you.
summary: Jesus Loves You is a student-led community where students strive to grow in their faith. We create a relaxed, judgment-free space for students to share, ask questions, and pursue Christ together, reminding one another of God's love for all of us.

name: Brazilian Portuguese Club
description: The mission of the TCU Portuguese Language Club is to create an inclusive community for students of all language levels and cultural backgrounds to learn about the Portuguese Language and Brazilian culture through conversations and fun cultural events. Join our GroupMe here!
summary: The Brazilian Portuguese Club offers a friendly space to practice Portuguese and explore Brazilian culture through casual conversation, interactive events, workshops, and movie nights. All language levels are welcome!

name: Equity Research Club at Neeley
description: The club exists to provide an inclusive, merit-based ecosystem for any TCU student, regardless of major or year, giving them the opportunity to develop practical, real-world finance skills. The ultimate goal is to accelerate their careers and position them to secure prestigious roles in high finance (like asset/wealth management, equity research, sales & trading, and hedge funds).
summary: NERC is an elite, C-Suite-led finance organization run like a real-world investment firm. We provide an advanced analyst program with hands-on training in financial modeling, valuation, and portfolio management to place members in top high-finance roles.

name: Still in Motion
description: The goal of Still in Motion is to create a welcoming, stress-free space where students can express themselves through movement, connect with others, and keep their love for dance alive throughout college. We will aim to build a supportive community where dancers of all backgrounds and skill levels can come together to move, grow, and simply enjoy the joy of dance—without pressure, judgment, or competition. Through shared experiences and creativity, we hope to form lasting friendships and a close-knit community united by expression and heart.
summary: We give dancers at TCU the opportunity to keep dancing throughout college without any pressure or judgement. We also provide the creative opportunity for TCU students to choreograph and express their own ideas and emotions through movement.

name: Investment Club
description:
The Investment Club aims to help students gain a deeper understanding of investments, financial markets, and personal finance that they can use throughout their lives. Our organization is committed to fostering an inclusive environment where students of all backgrounds and levels of experience can explore and engage in the vast world of investing.

 

Join our GroupMe to receive updates and get involved! https://groupme.com/join_group/112235355/357LQq4e

 

Follow us on Instagram! https://www.instagram.com/investmentclubtcu?igsh=MW10MW40cDN4OGltdg%3D%3D&utm_source=qr

 

Advisor: Layne Bradley
summary: To help students gain a deeper understanding of investments, financial markets, and personal finance to help make informed decisions about their futures.

name: National Black Law Student Association Pre-Law Chapter
description: The purpose of this organization is to educate Black and other minority pre-law students about the law school application process, legal education, and professional expectations. The organization also strives to connect students with lawyers, law schools, and professional networks that foster mentorship and career development. In addition, this organization serves as a community that encourages students to grow as ethical leaders and advocates for justice within the legal profession.
summary: Works to advance the law school application rate and admittance aspirations of Black and minority pre-law students, foster and cultivate skills, competencies, and strategies to excel academically in the law school environment.

name: Theta Tau
description: Theta Tau Psi is a professional engineering fraternity committed to building a supportive, collaborative, and service-driven community for engineering students at TCU. Our mission is to cultivate strong leaders through mentorship, professional development, academic support, and hands-on involvement. We host workshops, service events, social activities, and networking opportunities that help members grow both personally and professionally. By bringing together students from all engineering disciplines, we aim to create a space where future engineers can connect, learn, and make a positive impact on campus and beyond.
summary: Theta Tau Psi is a professional engineering fraternity at TCU focused on community, leadership, and academic and professional development. We bring engineers together through service, mentorship, and meaningful engagement.

name: Coalition of Occupational Therapy Advocates for Diversity TCU Chapter
description:
COTAD PROMO 2025

TCU-COTAD is a local chapter of the National Coalition of Occupational Therapy Advocates for Diversity (COTAD). Our organization shares the national mission “to empower occupational therapy leaders to engage in practices that promote justice, equity, diversity, and inclusion (JEDI), as well as anti-racism and anti-oppression, for a more transformative occupational therapy profession.”

Vision: To empower students to lead with cultural humility and advocate for justice, equity, diversity, and inclusion within occupational therapy and beyond.

Purpose:

  • Educate and advocate issues related to JEDI (justice, equity, diversity, and inclusion) in Occupational Therapy. 
  • Uphold TCU’s mission of community service through participation in service or community engagement projects that address justice, equity, diversity, and inclusion.
  • Foster leadership development and cultural humility among students in the OTD program.
  • Collaborate with other organizations at TCU to further the mission of COTAD.
summary: TCU COTAD promotes justice, equity, diversity, and inclusion within the field of occupational therapy by engaging in community service, fostering student leadership, and advancing education on cultural humility to build a more inclusive future.

name: Student Occupational Therapy Association
description: • Promotes interest in and further disseminate knowledge of occupational therapy within Texas Christian University and the community. SOTA fosters communication with Occupational Therapy students statewide and nationwide through involvement in the Texas Occupational Therapy Association and the Assembly of Student Delegates. The American Occupational Therapy Association is a nationally recognized organization that advocates for the occupational therapy profession and advances the quality and accessibility of occupational therapy services. The Student Occupational Therapy Association serves as the local student chapter within occupational therapy programs, dedicated to promoting the profession, increasing awareness of occupational therapy on campus, and fostering engagement, leadership, and professional development among OT students.
summary: SOTA at TCU promotes awareness of occupational therapy, supports professional development through leadership and service, and connects students locally and nationally within the OT community.

name: Ballroom and Latin Social Dance Organization
description: The purpose of the Ballroom and Latin Social Dance Organization is to create meaningful connections among students through the shared experience of movement. We believe social dance builds trust, communication, and confidence—offering a fun, low-pressure way to meet new people, develop interpersonal skills, and feel part of something bigger. By providing free weekly classes in Ballroom and Latin styles and inclusive events, we aim to make dance accessible to all, regardless of background or experience. Our purpose is to help students feel seen, supported, and connected—on and off the dance floor.
summary: Ballroom and Latin Social Dance Organization

name: Smile Ambassadors
description:
Smile Ambassadors is a student organization at TCU that connects pre-dental students with local dental clinics and oral health organizations to gain hands-on experience, explore the field of dentistry, and serve underserved communities. Members participate in group volunteer days where they assist with patient triage, sterilization, chairside support, and shadowing dental professionals, learning directly from those in the field. These in-person experiences allow students to build practical skills, collaborate with peers, and gain insight into clinical dental care, all while making a meaningful impact on the community. Open to students from all majors, Smile Ambassadors combines service, professional growth, and community, giving members the opportunity to develop as future healthcare leaders while promoting oral health across Fort Worth.

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/111418693/wT3QvrUd
summary: Dental and oral health community.

name: TCU Men’s Volleyball Club
description: The TCU Men’s Volleyball Club’s purpose is to provide men at TCU an opportunity to compete in a competitive environment, to represent TCU through athletics, to provide a community through a common interest, and to bring awareness of a growing sport in the state of Texas.
summary: Competitive men's indoor volleyball league.

name: Juntos en Cristo
description:
The purpose of Juntos en Cristo is to provide a Christ-centered community that uplifts
students through Scripture and fellowship, while creating meaningful cultural and
spiritual connections among TCU’s diverse student body. By encouraging bilingual
engagement, service, and shared worship experiences, the organization aims to foster
belonging, cultural appreciation, and spiritual growth.
summary: Spanish-speaking spiritual group.

name: Bridgepoint
description: Bridgepoint connects current students with alumni from at least 50 years to build meaningful relationships that celebrate the shared TCU experience. Through intentional conversations, mentorship, and storytelling, we foster community across generations of Horned Frogs. Our mission is to strengthen the bridge between TCU’s history and its future by inspiring connection, gratitude, and lifelong pride in the TCU legacy.
summary: Connecting TCU alumni to current students.

name: Athletes in Action
description: To see every athlete at TCU have an opportunity to become a “total” athlete who is growing physically, mentally and spiritually.
summary: to see every athlete at TCU have an opportunity to become a “total” athlete who is growing physically, mentally and spiritually.

name: Frogs Serving Fort Worth
description:
Mission

Our mission is to stand alongside Fort Worth’s unhoused community with compassion and respect—offering essential supplies, meaningful resources, and genuine care. We aim to foster connection, dignity, and hope through ongoing outreach, student leadership, and partnerships that create sustainable change.

Vision

We envision a compassionate TCU and Fort Worth community united in empathy, advocacy, and action. Frogs Serving Fort Worth aspires to bridge the gap between students and residents, building a city where every person is seen, valued, and supported. Our work reflects the values of leadership, faith, and social responsibility central to Texas Christian University.

Purpose

Frogs Serving Fort Worth mobilizes TCU students to engage in service efforts that meet immediate needs—such as distributing food, hygiene kits, and clothing—while also developing long-term relationships with individuals experiencing homelessness. Through these experiences, we raise campus awareness, support local nonprofits, and promote student leadership in community engagement and social justice.

Connection to TCU’s Mission

Frogs Serving Fort Worth brings TCU’s mission to life by empowering students to act as ethical leaders and responsible citizens through service. Every event and outreach initiative helps students grow in empathy, leadership, and understanding—making a tangible impact on Fort Worth while learning the value of community care.
summary: Frogs Serving Fort Worth is a student-led organization at Texas Christian University dedicated to serving the local homeless community through compassion, respect, and hands-on outreach.

name: Able Hearts Fellowship
description:
The purpose of Able Hearts Fellowship is to provide a safe and welcoming space where individuals of all abilities can connect through faith, friendship, and fun. We aim to bridge communities by hosting inclusive events, fostering meaningful relationships, and encouraging spiritual growth rooted in God’s love. Through service, worship, and shared experiences, we seek to reflect Christ’s heart for inclusion and remind every person that they are valued, loved, and have a purpose.

 

 

We aspire to create a world where every person, regardless of ability, is seen, included, and celebrated. Through Christ’s love, we inspire friendship, joy, and a community that embraces belonging for all.
summary: Able Hearts Fellowship is a Christ-driven community dedicated to building friendships and creating inclusive, joyful experiences for individuals with intellectual and physical disabilities across Fort Worth.

name: Neeley AI Leadership Club (N.A.I.L.)
description:
We're not here to hype AI. We're here to make sure you actually know how to use it. That means learning how to apply AI strategically across real business problems, accelerating your research, sharpening your work, and automating what doesn't need your brain so you can focus on what does.

 

Our belief is simple: AI should amplify human judgment, not replace it. The professionals who will matter most in the next decade aren't the ones who outsource their thinking to a chatbot. They're the ones who combine strong fundamentals with the ability to deploy AI as a genuine force multiplier.

 

The gap between students who understand AI and those who don't is already showing up in hiring. NAIL Club exists to make sure you're on the right side of that gap, with the skills, experience, and network to back it up.

 

NAIL Club is where that starts.
summary: AI is reshaping every business role. NAIL Club exists to make sure you're ready.

name: The Hippocratic Society at TCU
description: The Hippocratic Society is a community of undergraduate pre-health students who are committed to living out the highest ideals of the healing profession. Its purpose is to form and sustain clinicians who pursue and practice medicine grounded in moral integrity, sound judgment, and human flourishing. Through dialogue, and reflection, members cultivate virtues such as wisdom, courage, compassion, and integrity which are qualities essential to any healthcare profession.
summary: The Hippocratic Society exists to bridge the gap between science and humanity in pre-health education.

name: Science for Starters
description:
We empower children in underserved communities through volunteer-led STEM activities that ensure access to meaningful STEM learning.

By engaging TCU students as mentors, our organization provides hands-on science and math experiences that inspire confidence and a passion for discovery among children. At the same time, volunteers develop leadership skills and a strong commitment to community service, creating a positive and lasting impact for both the children we serve and the TCU students who serve them.
summary: A STEM-outreach organization that connects TCU students to elementary school children in underserved communities with the vision of improving access to STEM learning for every child.

name: Texas Conservation Initiative
description: The mission of Texas Conservation Initiative is to unite hunters, anglers, and outdoor enthusiasts at TCU to promote responsible wildlife management and conservation education. We aim to create a community of avid outdoorsmen and women protecting Texas' home waters and land, while sustainably participating in the hunting and fishing activities we love.
summary: Aiming to protect the land, respect the hunt, & preserve the waters.

name: Hawaiian and Pacific Islander Club
description: To celebrate the Hawaiian and Pacific Islander Culture with students at Texas Christian University through club meetings and organized events.
summary: To celebrate the Hawaiian and Pacific Islander Culture with students at Texas Christian University through club meetings and organized events.

name: Karaoke Club
description:
A place to sing, hang out and de-stress from classes. All are welcome. No singing experience required!

Our mission is to promote diversity and culture through sharing songs from around the world.

 

Instagram link: https://www.instagram.com/tcu.karaoke.club/
summary: Our mission is to promote diversity and culture through sharing songs from around the world.

name: Frogs Finance & Fundraising
description:
Frogs Finance and Fundraising hosts interactive workshops, fundraising campaigns, and service projects that connect financial education with real community impact. Members learn practical skills such as budgeting, investing basics, and nonprofit fundraising strategy. They apply these skills by organizing on-campus events and partnering with Fort Worth charities. Students gain hands-on leadership experience while using finance to make a meaningful difference.

Intro Video
summary: Frogs Finance & Fundraising (FFF) is a student-led club where members design and run fundraisers, support local nonprofits, and build real-world finance, leadership, and project skills through hands-on community impact.

name: Pre-Optometry Club
description: Host speakers, volunteer opportunities, and hold meetings to foster community in the field of optometry.
summary: Host speakers, volunteer opportunities, and hold meetings to foster community in the field of optometry.

name: Once Upon A Room at TCU
description:
Once Upon a Room (OUR) at TCU is a student-led service organization dedicated to bringing joy, comfort, and hope to hospitalized children through personalized hospital room makeovers and meaningful volunteer experiences. As part of the national Once Upon a Room network, our chapter partners with Cook Children’s Medical Center to support patients and families during long-term hospital stays. We empower students to serve with creativity, compassion, and purpose by transforming hospital rooms into uplifting, personalized spaces that inspire healing during difficult times. Through room decorating, day bag packing, hospital outreach events, and leadership opportunities, OUR provides hands-on service where students can see the impact of their volunteer work firsthand. make this like 50 words for a student organization page

Instagram: @onceuponaroomftw
summary: Once Upon a Room (OUR) at TCU partners with Cook Children’s Medical Center to bring joy and comfort to hospitalized children through room makeovers and outreach events, while giving students meaningful ways to serve and make a positive impact.

name: Horned Frog Digital Marketing
description:
Digital Marketing Club

Our vision is to develop future marketing leaders who redefine how brands connect with audiences in the digital world.

Our mission is to empower, educate, and inspire students to explore, create, and lead in the dynamic field of digital marketing. Through hands-on learning, professional development, and collaboration with industry professionals, we aim to provide our members with the skills, creativity, and confidence needed to thrive as future marketing professionals.
summary: Digital Marketing Club

name: Cybersecurity Club
description: Our mission is to connect learners of all backgrounds, encourage collaboration, and promote ethical practices in the cybersecurity field. We aspire to bridge the gap between academia and industry by fostering cybersecurity talent through competitive challenges, outreach initiatives, and professional partnerships that drive innovation and digital resilience. The Cybersecurity Club supports and advances the university’s mission by promoting cybersecurity awareness, technical skill development, and ethical digital practices. Through competitive challenges, collaboration, industry partnerships, and educational initiatives, members engage in creative problem-solving and service that prepare them to contribute meaningfully to the security and resilience of our interconnected world.
summary: We are a student-led community dedicated to building cybersecurity awareness, sharing knowledge, and developing technical skills that safeguard our digital world.

name: Baking Society
description: The goal of the TCU Baking Society is to create a welcoming and inclusive community built on a shared passion for baking. We bring students together through culinary creativity, promote mindful and health-conscious recipes and provide a space for learning and connection.
summary: The Baking Society

name: Accounting Club
description:
The Accounting Club at TCU is a student-led organization dedicated to helping Horned Frogs explore careers in accounting, build real professional skills, and connect directly with firms and alumni. We aim to create a clear pathway from the classroom to internships and full-time opportunities by hosting networking events, audit/tax/advisory workshops, speaker panels, recruiting prep sessions, and peer-to-peer support.

Our mission is simple: equip students with the knowledge, confidence, and connections needed to succeed in the accounting profession—whether they’re targeting the Big 4, regional firms, corporate finance, or graduate programs.
summary: Accounting Club

summary: Latin Graduate Leadership Network

name: Introduction to University Life (UNLF)
description: Introduction to University Life (UNLF) is a student success seminar for incoming TCU students to help them cultivate habits of learning and academic achievement. This one-credit hour, seminar-style course provides knowledge, skills, and strategies to help students successfully transition into academic life at TCU.
summary: Introduction to University Life (UNLF) is a student success seminar for incoming TCU students to help them cultivate habits of learning and academic achievement.

name: TCU Homecoming
description:
For decades, TCU has united fellow Horned Frogs, past and present, by holding an annual Homecoming celebration. Homecoming is historically held each October on a football game weekend with a variety of events and activities for the entire TCU Family. Participating student organizations will compete across multiple Homecoming categories, using the theme as their creative foundation throughout the week.

All Homecoming forms can be found on this TCU Engage page. For questions, please reach out to studentactivities@tcu.edu.
summary: TCU Homecoming Week Student Organization Competition
